children.heading: Authorizing committees
children.label: 1 Authorizing committees
children.text: The term “authorizing committees” means the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Combination of institutions of higher education
children.label: 2 Combination of institutions of higher education
children.text: The term “combination of institutions of higher education” means a group of institutions of higher education that have entered into a cooperative arrangement for the purpose of carrying out a common objective, or a public or private nonprofit agency, organization, or institution designated or created by a group of institutions of higher education for the purpose of carrying out a common objective on the group’s behalf.

children.heading: Critical foreign language
children.label: 3 Critical foreign language
children.text: Except as otherwise provided, the term “critical foreign language” means each of the languages contained in the list of critical languages designated by the Secretary in the Federal Register on (50 Fed. Reg. 31412; promulgated under the authority of section 212(d) of the Education for Economic Security Act (repealed by section 2303 of the Augustus F. Hawkins-Robert T. Stafford Elementary and Secondary School Improvement Amendments of 1988)), as updated by the Secretary from time to time and published in the Federal Register, except that in the implementation of this definition with respect to a specific title, the Secretary may set priorities according to the purposes of such title and the national security, economic competitiveness, and educational needs of the United States.August 2, 1985

children.heading: Department
children.label: 4 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Education.

children.label: i
children.text: offers, for a fee, degrees, diplomas, or certificates, that may be used to represent to the general public that the individual possessing such a degree, diploma, or certificate has completed a program of postsecondary education or training; and

children.label: ii
children.text: requires such individual to complete little or no education or coursework to obtain such degree, diploma, or certificate; and

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary pursuant to subpart 2 of part H of subchapter IV; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a Federal agency, State government, or other organization or association that recognizes accrediting agencies or associations.

children.heading: Disability
children.label: 6 Disability
children.text: The term “disability” has the same meaning given that term under .section 12102(2) of title 42

children.label: i
children.text: to deliver instruction to students who are separated from the instructor; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to support regular and substantive interaction between the students and the instructor, synchronously or asynchronously.

children.label: i
children.text: the Internet;

children.label: ii
children.text: one-way and two-way transmissions through open broadcast, closed circuit, cable, microwave, broadband lines, fiber optics, satellite, or wireless communications devices;

children.label: iii
children.text: audio conferencing; or

children.label: iv
children.text: video cassettes, DVDs, and CD–ROMs, if the cassettes, DVDs, or CD–ROMs are used in a course in conjunction with any of the technologies listed in clauses (i) through (iii).

children.label: A
children.text: a Head Start program or an Early Head Start program carried out under the Head Start Act ( et seq.), including a migrant or seasonal Head Start program, an Indian Head Start program, or a Head Start program or an Early Head Start program that also receives State funding;42 U.S.C. 9831

children.label: B
children.text: a State licensed or regulated child care program; or

children.label: i
children.text: serves children from birth through age six that addresses the children’s cognitive (including language, early literacy, and early mathematics), social, emotional, and physical development; and

children.label: I
children.text: a State prekindergarten program;

children.label: II
children.text: a program authorized under section 619 [] or part C of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act [ et seq.]; or20 U.S.C. 141920 U.S.C. 1431

children.label: III
children.text: a program operated by a local educational agency.

children.heading: Elementary school
children.label: 9 Elementary school
children.text: The term “elementary school” has the same meaning given that term under .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Gifted and talented
children.label: 10 Gifted and talented
children.text: The term “gifted and talented” has the same meaning given that term under .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Local educational agency
children.label: 11 Local educational agency
children.text: The term “local educational agency” has the same meaning given that term under .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: New borrower
children.label: 12 New borrower
children.text: The term “new borrower” when used with respect to any date means an individual who on that date has no outstanding balance of principal or interest owing on any loan made, insured, or guaranteed under subchapter IV.

children.heading: Nonprofit
children.label: 13 Nonprofit
children.text: The term “nonprofit” as applied to a school, agency, organization, or institution means a school, agency, organization, or institution owned and operated by one or more nonprofit corporations or associations, no part of the net earnings of which inures, or may lawfully inure, to the benefit of any private shareholder or individual.

children.heading: Poverty line
children.label: 14 Poverty line
children.text: The term “poverty line” means the poverty line (as defined in ) applicable to a family of the size involved.section 9902(2) of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: to prepare the students to become ministers of religion or to enter upon some other religious vocation (or to provide continuing training for any such vocation); or

children.label: B
children.text: to prepare the students to teach theological subjects.

children.heading: Secondary school
children.label: 16 Secondary school
children.text: The term “secondary school” has the same meaning given that term under .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 17 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Education.

children.heading: Service-learning
children.label: 18 Service-learning
children.text: The term “service-learning” has the same meaning given that term under section 12511(23) of title 42.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Special education teacher
children.label: 19 Special education teacher
children.text: The term “special education teacher” means teachers who teach children with disabilities as defined in section 602 of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act.

children.heading: State educational agency
children.label: 20 State educational agency
children.text: The term “State educational agency” has the same meaning given that term under .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: State
children.label: A State
children.text: The term “State” includes, in addition to the several States of the United States, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the District of Columbia, Guam, American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and the Freely Associated States.

children.heading: Freely Associated States
children.label: B Freely Associated States
children.text: The term “Freely Associated States” means the Republic of the Marshall Islands, the Federated States of Micronesia, and the Republic of Palau.

children.heading: State higher education agency
children.label: 22 State higher education agency
children.text: The term “State higher education agency” means the officer or agency primarily responsible for the State supervision of higher education.

children.heading: Universal design
children.label: 23 Universal design
children.text: The term “universal design” has the meaning given the term in .section 3002 of title 29

children.label: A
children.text: provides flexibility in the ways information is presented, in the ways students respond or demonstrate knowledge and skills, and in the ways students are engaged; and

children.label: B
children.text: reduces barriers in instruction, provides appropriate accommodations, supports, and challenges, and maintains high achievement expectations for all students, including students with disabilities and students who are limited English proficient.
